name: Green Tea
summary: An unoxidised tea rich in the catechin EGCG and the calming amino acid L-theanine — a combination studied for cholesterol and for steady, focused alertness.
superfoodReason: A near-calorie-free drink delivering EGCG and L-theanine, each with its own body of randomized-trial evidence for heart and cognitive effects.

benefits.mechanism: Green tea's catechins (chiefly EGCG) reduce how much cholesterol the gut absorbs and influence its handling by the liver. A meta-analysis of 14 randomized trials found green tea lowered total and LDL cholesterol.

citations.title: Green tea intake lowers fasting serum total and LDL cholesterol in adults: a meta-analysis of 14 randomized controlled trials (Zheng et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2011)

benefits.mechanism: Green tea pairs caffeine with L-theanine, an amino acid that increases alpha brain-wave activity. Taken together they improve attention and reaction time while blunting the jittery edge caffeine alone can cause.

citations.title: The combined effects of L-theanine and caffeine on cognitive performance and mood (Owen et al., Nutr Neurosci 2008)

benefits.claim: Regular drinkers show lower rates of cognitive impairment — an association, not a proven effect
benefits.mechanism: Green tea's polyphenols, chiefly EGCG, are antioxidant and anti-inflammatory in the lab, and are proposed to blunt the oxidative stress and neuroinflammation that accompany cognitive decline. A 2026 meta-analysis of 18 observational studies (58,929 participants) found green tea drinkers had lower odds of cognitive impairment (OR 0.63, 95% CI 0.54-0.73). The evidence is observational throughout, so the tea cannot be shown to be the cause, and the association was found in Asian but not European populations.

citations.title: The Association between Green Tea Consumption and Cognitive Function: A Meta-Analysis of Current Evidence (Zhou et al., Neuroepidemiology 2026)

surprisingFacts.text: L-theanine is found almost nowhere in the human diet except tea (and one rare mushroom), which is why tea's alertness feels different from coffee's.
